summary: W8 retry mechanism (server-side retry loop + WS broadcasts + UI progress chip + last_artifact inspect link) all functioned correctly end-to-end. However the heuristic verifier produced a FALSE POSITIVE: it rejected 3 consecutive functionally-complete Tetris artifacts because Qwen3-Coder chose DOM-grid rendering instead of <canvas>. The architect's pass criterion ('NO retry button click, NO debug, NO half-finished hand-off') was NOT met for this run.
what_worked: W8 retry loop fired exactly as designed (3 attempts, with attempt-2 + attempt-3 clearing prior artifacts/)
WS envelopes broadcast correctly (generate_attempt_started, generate_attempt_failed) and frontend rendered live progress chip 正在生成... (尝试 N/M)
Failure reasons surfaced in user-readable Chinese (missing_canvas: 找不到 <canvas> 元素 — 游戏类应用必须有画布)
On terminal failure, last_artifact inspect link was synthesized into the failure message and exposed in UI as 查看最后一次产物 ↗
POST /api/generate response shape on failure matches W8 spec: HTTP 500 + {kind: generate_quality_failed, reason: <joined reasons | last_artifact=path>}
Tape + CAS integrity holds: spec.md sha256 b19fe1fe… matches the CAS CAPSULE cid b19fe1fe…07a53a6f shown in UI
Server did NOT crash, did NOT panic, did NOT leak the API key, did NOT loop infinitely
what_failed: Heuristic Check 2 (has_canvas: lower.contains("<canvas")) is too strict — game frameworks routinely render via DOM grids, Three.js, WebGL, SVG, or other non-canvas surfaces

All 3 Qwen attempts consistently used DOM grid rendering; the verifier rejected all 3 even though each was a fully playable Tetris (200 cells, 7 tetrominoes, keyboard handler, animation loop, localStorage, game-over)
User-facing outcome: failure UI is shown despite the artifact being functional. User must click 查看最后一次产物 link to discover working game — this is NOT a 'clean delivery'
Final 500 response body does NOT include total_attempts field (only the success path includes it); frontend recovers attempts from WS log, but the API contract is asymmetric

Tape layout side-finding: sessions/<id>/ was written to cwd /Users/zephryj/work/turingosv4/ not tmp/phase7_active/ — workspace override appears not effective for this server PID. Suggests environment-variable plumbing for TURINGOS_WEB_WORKSPACE may need verification (out of W8 scope)

round_1.qwen_first_attempt: produced broken Tetris with player.matrix === null inverted nullish guard pattern (top-of-game frozen)
round_1.user_recovery: user-simulator manually triggered POST /api/generate a second time and got a working Tetris on attempt 2

w8_round.w8_outcome: all 3 rejected by heuristic; 500 returned; user sees failure UI + inspect link
w8_round.user_experience: no manual retry needed to SEE the artifact (inspect link works), but the user is shown a failure UI for a working artifact — confusing

comparative_round1_vs_w8.qualitative_assessment: W8 substrate is sound but the heuristic verifier needs Check 2 relaxed. Round 1 ended with a working Tetris (after manual retry). W8 ended with a working Tetris (accessible via inspect link) AND a misleading failure UI. Net UX: arguably WORSE for non-developer end users because the failure message would lead them to either click 重试生成代码 (burning more SiliconFlow ¥) or abandon the task.

recommendation_to_architect: Do not ship W8 in current form. Specific follow-ups: (1) relax verify.rs Check 2 to accept either <canvas OR a DOM grid with sufficient cells (e.g., a div with >= 100 .cell descendants), OR a <svg> root, OR a WebGL context. (2) ensure final POST /api/generate failure response includes total_attempts field for symmetry. (3) when terminal failure occurs but last_artifact exists, consider passing the artifact through the iframe sandbox viewer with a warning chip instead of dumping user to a top-level page (W6 sandbox invariant). (4) separate concern: investigate why TURINGOS_WEB_WORKSPACE was not honored — sessions/ went to cwd.
